scope: Literature and priority audit for proposed WOW-284 extensions; the submitted manuscript is unchanged.
interpretation_rule: An unsuccessful search is not evidence of novelty or nonexistence.

topics.boundary: Aouchiche and Hansen's peer-reviewed survey records WOW-284 as Conjecture 7.16 and attributes it to Fajtlowicz's 1998 Written on the Wall report. It is the primary published source used here for the formal conjecture statement.

Located sources concern intact Moore distance spectra, adjacency perturbations, distance-critical deletion, or subconstituents. No direct indexed source stating the recomputed ordinary distance spectra of the one-vertex, adjacent-pair, or nonadjacent-pair punctures was located. Project derivations exist, but the nonadjacent-pair theorem remains subject to the direct-sum and multiplicity audit required before v2.

The upper endpoint of the regular WOW adjacency window is a spectral-Moore second-eigenvalue constraint. WOW additionally imposes a lower-tail constraint, so one-sided spectral-Moore results do not settle the conjecture.

Distance-polynomial, quotient-polynomial, predistance-polynomial, and minimal-cage frameworks already contain the adjacency-polynomial mechanism. Only the WOW specialization should be presented as the project contribution.

No indexed complete census was located for all connected 6-regular girth-five diameter-three graphs in the candidate order range. Structured-subclass generation and spectral order bounds exist and must be applied before any new exhaustive search.

The 9-regular order-96 graph and its adjacency list are publicly supplied by the constructor. Its WOW equality and distance spectrum are project-side exact computations and require a provenance-checked local data copy before v2.
The constructions are known. A diameter-three Fourier obstruction is a specialized project result with unresolved priority and does not cover higher-diameter members.

QEC controls the maximum distance quadratic form on the all-ones orthogonal complement, whereas WOW uses the least unrestricted distance eigenvalue. No direct implication or dual-degree link was located.

Near-Moore diameter-three Cayley families and one-sided spectral-Moore bounds do not provide the full two-sided shifted adjacency window required by WOW. No unconditional infinite family was located. Any infinite regular counterexample family must have unbounded degree, since strictness gives diameter less than k and the Moore bound leaves only finitely many graphs for each fixed k.
Regular and structured-subclass generators do not amount to a census of all irregular connected triangle- and 4-cycle-free graphs through order 37. Minimality remains unproved.
